input.request_text_preview: Replicate the research paper 'All In One': The paper "All-in-one simulation-based inference" has been reproduced. This involves 5 major phases and 233 total subtasks. The workflow includes: Variance Exploding SDE (VESDE) is implemented, as described in A2.1; The Simformer model, training process and

method: LLM agent selection + greedy fleet assignment
llm_reasoning: This is a research replication workflow for 'All-in-One SBI' with clearly defined phases (VESDE, Simformer model/training/inference, and three baseline methods NPE/NRE/NLE). Given the intent classification shows moderate complexity (0.43) but low decomposability (0.30), the phases have tight interdependencies — baselines and Simformer share the same SBI framework and must be validated against common benchmarks. The workflow is best structured as: literature extraction → experiment design → parallel code generation tracks (Simformer vs. baselines) → integrated testing → result analysis and reporting.

llm_agent_details.name: literature_reviewer
llm_agent_details.prompt: Review the paper 'All-in-One Simulation-Based Inference' and extract: (1) Full VESDE formulation from Appendix A2.1 — SDE coefficients, noise schedules, drift/diffusion terms; (2) Simformer architecture details — transformer blocks, attention mechanisms, embedding dims, conditioning strategy; (3) Training procedure — loss functions, optimizer settings, batch sizes, learning rate schedules, number of epochs; (4) Inference procedure — posterior sampling steps, MCMC or score-based sampling details; (5) Baseline method configurations for NPE, NRE, and NLE — network architectures, training setups, and any shared components; (6) Benchmark tasks used for evaluation and the metrics reported (e.g., C2ST, log-probability, coverage). Output a structured specification document.
llm_agent_details.role: Extract all mathematical specifications, hyperparameters, and algorithmic details from the All-in-One SBI paper

llm_agent_details.name: experiment_designer
llm_agent_details.prompt: Using the extracted paper specifications, design a complete replication experiment plan for All-in-One SBI. Define: (1) The 5 major experimental phases and their subtask breakdown (targeting ~233 subtasks); (2) The benchmark tasks (e.g., SLCP, Lotka-Volterra, Gaussian mixture) with simulator configs; (3) A shared data/simulator interface so Simformer, NPE, NRE, and NLE all run on identical draws; (4) Evaluation protocol — number of posterior samples, reference posterior generation, metric computation (C2ST, MMD, or log-prob); (5) Directory and file structure for reproducibility (configs, checkpoints, results); (6) Which phases can run in parallel and which are sequential. Output a phased experiment plan with dependency graph.
llm_agent_details.role: Design the full replication experiment plan covering all 5 phases and integration of Simformer vs. baselines

llm_agent_details.name: code_generator
llm_agent_details.prompt: Implement the following components from the All-in-One SBI paper: (1) VESDE (Variance Exploding SDE) as specified in Appendix A2.1 — implement the forward SDE, reverse SDE, and score network target; (2) Simformer architecture — transformer-based amortized score network that conditions on observed data x_o, implement multi-head attention, positional encodings, and the joint embedding of (theta, x, t); (3) Training loop — denoising score matching loss over simulated (theta, x) pairs with time-conditional noise injection following the VESDE schedule; (4) Inference — implement posterior sampling via reverse SDE or predictor-corrector sampler conditioned on x_o; Use PyTorch. Follow the exact hyperparameters from the experiment design spec. Include docstrings and config-driven hyperparameters.
llm_agent_details.role: Implement VESDE and the full Simformer model, training loop, and score-based inference pipeline

llm_agent_details.name: code_gen
llm_agent_details.prompt: Implement the three baseline methods for the All-in-One SBI replication: (1) Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE) — normalizing flow (e.g., MAF or NSF) trained on (theta, x) pairs to directly approximate p(theta | x); (2) Neural Ratio Estimation (NRE) — binary classifier trained to estimate the likelihood-to-evidence ratio; (3) Neural Likelihood Estimation (NLE) — normalizing flow trained to approximate p(x | theta), combined with MCMC for posterior; For each: implement training, posterior evaluation, and a common interface `sample_posterior(x_obs, n_samples)`. Use the same benchmark simulators as Simformer. Ensure all three share the same train/test data splits defined in the experiment design. Use PyTorch; sbi library wrappers are acceptable if they match paper configs.
llm_agent_details.role: Implement the three baseline SBI methods: NPE, NRE, and NLE using sbi library or from-scratch as specified

llm_agent_details.name: tester
llm_agent_details.prompt: Write a comprehensive test suite for the All-in-One SBI replication codebase: (1) Unit tests for VESDE — verify SDE coefficients, check that forward noising pushes samples toward noise distribution, validate score network output shape; (2) Unit tests for Simformer — check forward pass shapes, attention mask correctness, conditioning on x_o; (3) Integration test: run a short training loop (10 steps) on a toy Gaussian simulator and verify loss decreases; (4) Baseline sanity tests — verify NPE/NRE/NLE each produce posterior samples of correct shape and that log-probs are finite; (5) Benchmark smoke test — run all 4 methods (Simformer + 3 baselines) on a single SLCP observation and confirm posterior samples are returned without error; Use pytest. Flag any numerical instability or shape mismatches.
llm_agent_details.role: Generate integration tests and unit tests for VESDE, Simformer, and all three baselines

llm_agent_details.name: experiment_runner
llm_agent_details.prompt: Execute the full replication experiments for All-in-One SBI across all benchmark tasks defined in the experiment plan: (1) Run Simformer training and inference on each benchmark (e.g., SLCP, Lotka-Volterra, Gaussian Mixture, Two Moons); (2) Run NPE, NRE, and NLE on the same benchmarks with identical simulator budgets; (3) For each method × task combination, collect: posterior samples (N≥1000), compute C2ST scores against reference posteriors, log-probability of true parameters, and wall-clock training time; (4) Save all results to structured output files (JSON/CSV per task); (5) Log any failures or numerical issues with traceback. Report a results matrix: rows=tasks, columns=methods, cells=metrics.
llm_agent_details.role: Execute all benchmark experiments for Simformer and baselines, collect posterior samples and metrics

llm_agent_details.name: result_analyzer
llm_agent_details.prompt: Analyze the experimental results from the All-in-One SBI replication run: (1) Compare C2ST scores, log-probabilities, and coverage metrics for Simformer vs. NPE, NRE, NLE across all benchmark tasks — tabulate against paper-reported values from Table 1/2; (2) Compute relative error between replicated metrics and paper metrics; flag any task where deviation exceeds 5%; (3) Identify which components replicate faithfully vs. show discrepancies (e.g., VESDE sampling, baseline training stability); (4) Check if the 'all-in-one' advantage of Simformer (single model, multiple tasks) is reproduced; (5) Summarize statistical validity — are differences within expected variance given random seeds? Output a scored replication report with pass/fail per major claim.
llm_agent_details.role: Compare Simformer vs. baseline results against paper-reported numbers and score replication fidelity

llm_agent_details.name: reporter
llm_agent_details.prompt: Write a comprehensive replication report for 'All-in-One Simulation-Based Inference': (1) Executive summary — was the paper successfully replicated? Overall verdict (Full/Partial/Failed); (2) Methods section — brief description of VESDE, Simformer, and baselines as implemented; (3) Results tables — replicated metrics vs. paper metrics for all benchmark × method combinations; (4) Discrepancy analysis — explain any gaps, likely causes (missing hyperparams, stochasticity, compute differences); (5) Phase completion summary — confirm all 5 phases and ~233 subtasks status; (6) Recommendations for future replication or extension. Format as a structured Markdown report suitable for inclusion in a research log.
llm_agent_details.role: Produce the final replication report summarizing methodology, results, discrepancies, and reproducibility verdict
